name: browser
description: Self-hosted browser automation via Browser Jet Pilot. Connects to your own Chromium instance through CDP — no third-party browser services needed. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, fill forms, take screenshots, extract web content, or automate any browser task.

session.note: ALWAYS call browser_start before any other tool. Call browser_end when done.

navigation.note: Use browser_navigate to go to URLs. Use browser_get_info to check current state.

observation.note: Observe before acting. Use browser_get_content for text, browser_screenshot for visuals, browser_evaluate for JS, browser_wait_for for dynamic content.

interaction.note: Prefer browser_fill for forms. Use browser_type only when keystroke events are needed. Always wait for elements before interacting.

shader.note: Use browser_disable_shaders before navigating to heavy WebGL/shader pages. Blocks WebGL, freezes CSS animations, throttles requestAnimationFrame.

always: Call browser_start as the first step in every browser session
Use browser_get_content or browser_screenshot to observe the page before taking action
Use browser_wait_for when pages load content dynamically (SPAs, lazy loading)
Handle errors gracefully — try alternative selectors or wait longer if a tool fails
Save important screenshots to the user when visual verification is relevant
prefer: browser_fill over browser_type for form inputs (faster and more reliable)
browser_get_content over browser_screenshot for text extraction
browser_evaluate for extracting structured data (JSON, arrays, tables)
CSS selectors over XPath (simpler and more readable)
avoid: Clicking elements without first verifying they exist via browser_get_content or browser_wait_for
Calling browser_navigate without a complete, valid URL
More than 5 scroll steps in a row (use browser_evaluate to jump to sections instead)
Relying on screenshots to read text content — use browser_get_content instead
data_extraction: For single values: use browser_get_content with a CSS selector to scope the extraction
For paginated content: scroll, extract, click next, repeat — track items to avoid duplicates
For JSON data in pages: use browser_evaluate to find and parse <script> tags or API responses

examples.task: Go to a website and extract its title
Extract all links from a page

errorRecovery.element_not_found: Try browser_get_content to see what's actually on the page. The DOM structure may differ from expected. Try broader selectors or browser_evaluate to find the correct selector.
errorRecovery.timeout: The page may be slow. Increase browser_wait_for timeout to 30000ms. For SPA navigation, wait for specific elements rather than relying on navigate completion.
errorRecovery.navigation_failed: Check the URL is valid and complete (include https://). If the site blocks automated browsers, try browser_evaluate to check for CAPTCHAs or blocks.
errorRecovery.session_lost: Call browser_start again to reconnect. If the CDP connection dropped, check that the Chromium process is still running with --remote-debugging-port=9222.

output.text: Return extracted text content, page titles, form data, and any structured data as formatted text.
output.screenshots: Save screenshots as PNG files. Provide the file path to the user. Use base64 data from browser_screenshot tool results.
output.data: For structured extraction (tables, lists, JSON), format as clean JSON or markdown tables.
output.reports: For multi-step research tasks, compile results into a summary with supporting screenshots and extracted data.
